items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: The council reviewed status reports on San José's Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. The city also presented an updated Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, signaling potential changes to library operations and physical spaces.

items.headline: Project at 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing delayed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a development project at 333 Franklin Street has been postponed from April 8 and will be rescheduled for a later date. Residents who wanted to weigh in on this project should watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating continued work on this intersection safety improvement.

items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state grant for arts school
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza, expanding cultural programming in the city.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with revised criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including edits to the plan and new scoring criteria for evaluating projects. This plan will shape how Cupertino prioritizes biking, walking, and transit improvements over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council Proposes Changes to Vacancy Filling Process
items.summary: The council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled, including a potential reduction in the current process. This could affect how quickly the city fills empty council seats in the future.

items.headline: 65-unit senior affordable housing complex approved on E St John
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will add 65 affordable units specifically for seniors near downtown San José, addressing a critical housing gap for older residents. At nearly $8.8M, this is the largest single residential project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition sues city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: The city is in litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. This case indicates an ongoing dispute between residents and the city over traffic or pedestrian safety measures on that street.
